title: thymus pulegioides subsp. pulchellus
intro: Thymus pulegioides subsp. pulchellus, also known as the pink wood thyme, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the thyme family. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. Thymus pulegioides subsp. pulchellus has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies and traditional medicine.
